title: Draconopteris grandidentata

The classification of Draconopteris grandidentata is also associated with the class Polypodiopsida, which includes modern and fossil ferns. intro: Draconopteris grandidentata, also known as the Chinese dragon-scale fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive fronds with multiple divisions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Draconopteris grandidentata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or powder, and is also used in herbal teas and infusions.
